text: Confirm what you're seeing: `ps aux | grep defunct` (or `ps -eo pid,ppid,stat,cmd | grep ' Z'`). Zombies aren't running processes consuming CPU/memory -- they're just leftover table entries waiting for their parent to collect their exit status.
Check who their PPID points at: `ps -eo pid,ppid,stat,cmd | awk '$3 ~ /Z/ {print $2}' | sort -u`. That PID is the parent that keeps forking children and never calling `wait()` on them.
Identify that parent process: `ps -p <ppid> -o pid,cmd`. It's `batch-dispatcher`, running in an infinite loop, forking a new short-lived worker on an interval.
There's no config fix here -- the dispatcher's reaping bug means every run leaks. Stop it: `sudo pkill -f /usr/local/bin/batch-dispatcher`.
